name: org.machanism.machai:bindex-core
description: Bindex Core (org.machanism.machai:bindex-core) is the core Java library in the Machai ecosystem for working with Bindex metadata documents that describe software artifacts for discovery, recommendation, and AI-assisted integration. The library provides MongoDB-backed persistence for serialized bindex.json payloads, semantic library registration, vector-embedding generation based on artifact classification, and natural-language library lookup for automated project assembly workflows. Its main runtime responsibilities are centered on org.machanism.machai.bindex.MongoBindexRepository, which stores and retrieves Bindex documents from MongoDB, and org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker, which classifies free-text requirements, generates embeddings, filters by languages and architectural layers, performs vector search, and returns matching libraries together with dependency relationships. The module also exposes AI-oriented function tools through org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools so LLM-driven workflows can fetch Bindex records, inspect the Bindex schema, register bindex.json files, and request recommended libraries on demand. This artifact is intended for infrastructure-heavy Java applications, developer tooling, build automation, metadata indexing pipelines, and GenAI-assisted software assembly scenarios where searchable, machine-readable library metadata is required.

constructors.description: Creates a repository instance backed by a MongoDB collection. The configurator is used to resolve repository settings such as BINDEX_REPO_URL, and the repository then provides storage and retrieval access for serialized org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex documents. This constructor is the standard entry point when an application needs direct database-level access to registered Bindex records.

examples.description: Creates the repository, loads a registered Bindex by logical identifier, and closes the MongoDB client resources after use.

constructors.description: Creates a picker backed by the configured Bindex repository and a named GenAI provider. This constructor prepares the semantic search workflow used to classify user requests, generate embeddings, query MongoDB vector indexes, and return matching org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex records. It is the main entry point for AI-driven library recommendation and registration scenarios.

examples.description: Creates the semantic picker, adjusts the minimum accepted score, and queries for libraries that fit a natural-language requirement.

features.name: create
features.description: Registers or replaces a Bindex entry in the repository. During registration, the implementation serializes the org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex document, stores searchable metadata fields, computes an embedding from the classification section, and persists the result into MongoDB so the artifact can participate in semantic search and recommendation workflows.

examples.description: Builds a minimal Bindex document with semantic classification data and registers it so it becomes searchable in the Bindex repository.

features.name: pick
features.description: Picks matching Bindex entries for a natural-language query. The method uses a GenAI provider to classify the request, generates embeddings from the resulting classifications, applies repository filters such as language normalization and architectural layers, performs vector search in MongoDB, and returns the matching Bindex documents that best satisfy the query.

examples.description: Demonstrates how to perform semantic library selection for a real project need and iterate over the matching results.

features.name: getBindex
features.description: Retrieves a Bindex instance from the database by its logical Bindex identifier. The repository reads the serialized JSON payload from the dedicated MongoDB field and deserializes it into org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex so callers can inspect or reuse the registered metadata definition.

examples.description: Shows how an application can look up a previously registered library definition and inspect its metadata.

features.name: applyTools
features.description: Registers Bindex-related function tools with the provided GenAI provider. This enables LLM-assisted workflows to invoke operations such as retrieving a Bindex, reading the Bindex schema, registering a bindex.json file, and asking for recommended libraries without hardcoding those operations into the calling application.

examples.description: Attaches the Bindex tool set to an existing GenAI provider so assistant workflows can use Bindex-specific functions during execution.

customizations.name: BINDEX_REPO_URL
customizations.description: Configuration property used to override the MongoDB repository connection URI for Bindex storage and retrieval. Applications set this when they want Bindex Core to use a specific MongoDB deployment instead of the default configured endpoint.

examples.description: Configures the MongoDB connection URI on Windows before running Bindex retrieval or registration workflows.

customizations.name: BINDEX_REG_PASSWORD
customizations.description: Configuration or environment property that enables authenticated Bindex registration. When present, registration workflows can delete, replace, and insert Bindex records in MongoDB using credentials with write permissions.

examples.description: Sets the registration password required before calling registration-oriented workflows such as org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker.create(org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex).

customizations.name: Function tools integration via org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools
customizations.description: Service-style extension point that exposes Bindex capabilities to GenAI engines. Developers can wire this component into a provider so AI assistants gain access to schema lookup, Bindex retrieval, recommendation, and registration operations as callable tools.

examples.description: Demonstrates how to add Bindex-specific callable tools to a GenAI provider used by an assistant or automation runtime.

examples.title: Use Bindex Core in a Maven project for semantic library discovery
examples.scenario: How to install the library, configure required services, and run a semantic lookup for libraries that match a natural-language requirement.
steps: 1) Add the Maven dependency for org.machanism.machai:bindex-core:1.1.15 to your application project.
2) Ensure your application provides a Configurator implementation for GenAI and repository settings.
3) Configure MongoDB access with BINDEX_REPO_URL if you use a custom cluster and provide any required provider credentials for the selected GenAI implementation.
4) Create org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker, optionally call setScore(java.lang.Double), and then invoke pick(java.lang.String) with a realistic requirement.
5) Inspect the returned org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex results and select the libraries appropriate for your project.

configuration.notes: Use a valid GenAI provider configuration in your application runtime. BINDEX_REG_PASSWORD is not required for read-only semantic lookup scenarios.

examples.title: Register a bindex.json file through Bindex Core in an LLM-assisted workflow
examples.scenario: How to install the component, configure registration settings, and invoke the ready-to-use register_bindex capability in a practical assistant-driven setup.
steps: 1) Add org.machanism.machai:bindex-core:1.1.15 to your Maven project so the Bindex function tools are available at runtime.
2) Place a valid bindex.json file in the project working directory that the assistant or host application will use.
3) Configure BINDEX_REPO_URL for the target MongoDB repository and BINDEX_REG_PASSWORD for write access.
4) Initialize your GenAI provider and apply org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools so the register_bindex tool becomes available.
5) Invoke the register_bindex tool with fileName set to "bindex.json" and use the returned record identifier to confirm successful registration.

configuration.notes: The working directory used by the host application must contain the bindex.json file because the tool reads the file from that location before registration.
